report_name: Ransomware Attack on West Pharmaceutical Services Disrupts Global Operations

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The company's systems were impacted by a ransomware attack, which by definition involves encrypting data for impact. The proactive shutdown was intended to prevent widespread data encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely attempted to delete volume shadow copies to prevent system recovery, a common ransomware tactic. Hunting hints suggested looking for 'wmic.exe deleting shadow copies (vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet)'.

attack_patterns.name: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
attack_patterns.description: The attackers may have used SMB shares for lateral movement, as indicated by hunting hints to monitor for a sudden increase in SMB traffic between workstations.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: The WMI command-line utility was likely used to delete volume shadow copies as part of the ransomware attack's effort to inhibit system recovery.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: PsExec was mentioned as a potential tool for lateral movement within the victim's network.

identities.name: West Pharmaceutical Services
identities.description: A global manufacturer of pharmaceutical packaging and drug delivery systems that suffered a ransomware attack, leading to the shutdown of its infrastructure and disruption of global operations.

indicators.name: Ransomware Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Ransomware Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8092da-b191-4b35-8640-1135694c345b
status: experimental
description: Detects commands used to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.kaseya.com/blog/2026/05/20/the-week-in-breach-news-may-20-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_vssadmin:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  selection_wmic:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: 1 of them
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though rare.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe or wmic.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a behavior strongly associated with ransomware attacks to prevent recovery.

indicators.name: Ransomware Note File Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Ransomware Note File Creation
id: 9c3e1f2a-6b8d-4c7e-9a1b-0d2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a file named 'ransom.txt', which is a common name for ransomware notes.
references:
  - https://www.kaseya.com/blog/2026/05/20/the-week-in-breach-news-may-20-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\ransom.txt'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a file named 'ransom.txt', a common indicator that ransomware has been executed on a system.

indicators.name: PsExec Execution For Lateral Movement
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: PsExec Execution
id: 5d8a9b4c-1e2f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a legitimate tool often abused by attackers for lateral movement.
references:
  - https://www.kaseya.com/blog/2026/05/20/the-week-in-breach-news-may-20-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.lateral_movement
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    OriginalFileName: 'psexec.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use by system administrators.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a tool that can be used for lateral movement as suggested in the report's hunting hints.
